neighborhoods.name: L'Ecusson (Historic Center)
neighborhoods.description: Medieval city center with narrow pedestrian streets, Place de la Comedie, and abundant cafes and restaurants
neighborhoods.character: Dense urban historic, car-free core, vibrant student and cultural energy

housing.predominantType: Apartments in historic Haussmann and medieval buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: Place de la Comedie is the social heart. Tram lines converge here. Very lively but can be noisy at night.

neighborhoods.name: Antigone / Port Marianne
neighborhoods.description: Modern neo-classical district designed by Ricardo Bofill, extending east from the center along the Lez river to newer Port Marianne
neighborhoods.character: Modern, spacious, planned urban with waterfront, newer construction

housing.predominantType: Modern apartments with balconies, many with river or park views

neighborhoods.character_notes: Odysseum shopping complex at the east end. Newer buildings mean better insulation and accessibility. Tram Lines 1 and 3.

neighborhoods.name: Les Aubes / Aiguelongue
neighborhoods.description: Residential hillside neighborhood north of the center, near the zoo and Lunaret park, with green surroundings
neighborhoods.character: Quiet residential, green, family-friendly, close to nature

housing.predominantType: Villas and houses with gardens, some apartment buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: Adjacent to Parc Zoologique de Lunaret (free zoo). Good for dog owners with trails and green space. Bus service to center.

neighborhoods.name: Palavas-les-Flots
neighborhoods.description: Small beach town 12km south of Montpellier, directly on the Mediterranean with a canal-lined center
neighborhoods.character: Coastal resort, seasonal, small-town feel, beach lifestyle

housing.predominantType: Seaside apartments, many with sea views or canal-front

neighborhoods.character_notes: Tram Line 3 connects to Montpellier center in 30 minutes. Very touristy in summer. Dog-friendly beaches in off-season.

neighborhoods.name: Castelnau-le-Lez
neighborhoods.description: Affluent suburban commune east of Montpellier, well-connected by tram with a village center and the Lez river
neighborhoods.character: Upscale suburban, village center with amenities, good schools

housing.predominantType: Villas, townhouses, and newer apartment complexes

neighborhoods.character_notes: Tram Line 2 provides direct center access. Riverside walking paths along the Lez. Good supermarkets and medical facilities.
